summary: Folic acid supplementation is crucial preconceptionally and preferably starts at least three to four months prior to pregnancy planning due to its role in preventing neural tube defects; thus, it would be advisable to initiate this supplement well ahead if she plans any future pregnancies after marriage or even as part of general health maintenance given her age and medication use which may affect folate levels indirectly through metabolism changes induced by anti-epileptic drugs. The exact timing can vary based on individual circumstances but generally aiming towards starting around one month post-marriage could serve as an initial guideline while considering broader reproductive timelines into account. Agent1 concludes advising initiation closer to when actual family building intentions are clear rather than waiting indefinitely since optimal benefits accrue from consistent intake beginning several
